prompt: Why do computer-use agents need specialized RL environments and mixed action spaces?
reference_answer: Computer-use agents need specialized RL environments because desktop tasks are long-horizon and expensive to supervise with pure outcome-only signals. They also need mixed action spaces because real computer tasks combine GUI actions with higher-level operations or APIs. ComputerRL is important because it packages online RL with hybrid actions and scalable desktop environments instead of treating computer use as plain text generation.

documents.title: ComputerRL paper digest
documents.content: ComputerRL presents end-to-end online reinforcement learning for computer-use agents. The paper emphasizes hybrid API and GUI action spaces together with scalable parallel desktop environments so long interactive tasks become trainable.

documents.title: ComputerRL repository digest
documents.content: The ComputerRL repository describes computer-use training around mixed actions, realistic desktop environments, and online reinforcement learning instead of purely text-only supervision.

documents.title: OSWorld benchmark digest
documents.content: OSWorld is a computer-use benchmark built around real desktop tasks with long interactive trajectories. Such trajectories make scalable environments and action design central to training quality.

required_claims.text: Computer-use agents need mixed or hybrid action spaces because real tasks involve both GUI behavior and higher-level operations.
Specialized RL environments matter because desktop trajectories are long and expensive enough that scalable environments become important.
ComputerRL is important because it combines online RL with hybrid actions and realistic desktop environments instead of text-only supervision.
